title: Regulation of Land Tenure (Established Villages) Act
title_en: Regulation of Land Tenure (Established Villages) Act

provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act may be cited as the Regulation of Land Tenure (Established Villages) Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Act unless the context requires otherwise— " High Court " means the High Court (Land Division); " Minister " means the Minister for the time being responsible for lands; " Operation Vijiji " means the settlement or re-settlement of people in villages during and at any time between the years 1970 and 1977, for the purpose of implementing the policy of villagisation; " Tribunal " means the District Land and Housing Tribunal; " village land " means all the land within the boundaries of any village established as the result of Operation Vijiji , whether or not such area has been registered as a village in accordance with section 22 of the Local Government District Authorities) Act 1 . 1 Cap. 287

provisions.title: Extinction of certain rights
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of section 4 of this Act, all rights to occupy or to use land in accordance with any custom or rule of customary law existing or held or claimed to be held by any person in any Village land prior to operation Vijiji, are hereby extinguished. (2) For the avoidance of doubt the extinction of rights under subsection (1) of this section shall not affect— (a) any right to occupy or to use any village land which was acquired by any person during or subsequent to operation Vijiji, in any village established as the result of Operation Vijiji , or (b) any right to use or to occupy any land in accordance with any custom or rule of customary law existing in any village which was not established as the result of operation Vijiji.

provisions.title: Claims for compensation
provisions.content: Where in any proceedings instituted in accordance with the provisions of this Act, it is proved to the satisfaction of the Tribunal that— (a) any person was an occupier of any village land to which section 3 applies; (b) the person had made any unexhausted improvements or added any ascertainable value to the village land prior to operation Vijiji; and (c) such land was not subsequently allocated to that person, the Tribunal may award such compensation as the Tribunal may think is fair in relation to the value of the unexhausted improvements or the value added to that land.

provisions.title: Institution of proceedings
provisions.content: (1) Proceedings under this Act may be instituted in the Tribunal having jurisdiction over the area in which the dispute arises: Provided that where the Customary Leaseholds (Enfranchisement) Act 2 does not apply, the Minister may for the purpose of this Act, establish a Tribunal and the Tribunal so established shall perform all the functions and exercise all the powers of the Tribunal under this Act. 2 Cap. 377

provisions.title: Revocation of G.N. No. 88 of 1987
provisions.content: [Revokes the Extinction of Customary Land Rights Order] History of this document 31 December 2023 Chapter 267 Revised Laws 2023 Consolidation 10 May 2013 amendment not yet applied Amended by Written Laws (Miscellaneous Amendments) Act, 2013 31 July 2002 this version Chapter 267 Revised Laws 2002 Consolidation 01 January 1993 Commenced Related documents Is amended by Written Laws (Miscellaneous Amendments) Act, 2013 More documents like this one [] [] [] [{"provision_eid": "sec_3", "citations": 3}, {"provision_eid": "sec_3__subsec_1", "citations": 1}, {"provision_eid": "sec_4", "citations": 2}, {"provision_eid": "sec_5", "citations": 3}, {"provision_eid": "sec_6", "citations": 4}, {"provision_eid": "sec_9", "citations": 1}] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
